risks.failure_mode: A skeptical or panel prompt catches more hacks by treating every unusual diff as malicious.
risks.mitigation: Run paired controls in the same gate and fail if specificity drops below 0.90.

risks.failure_mode: Source split, static-survivor status, official reports, or known-both-miss language leaks.
risks.mitigation: Freeze prompt allowlists and run a substring/key leakage audit before calls.

risks.failure_mode: Panel aggregation is chosen after seeing outputs.
risks.mitigation: Pre-register all panel rules and report each rule, including bad ones.

risks.failure_mode: Precision looks perfect because the current model catches only three hacks.
risks.mitigation: Always report denominator, recall, specificity, and balanced detection together.

risks.failure_mode: Execution-backed verification expands into unbounded cloud work.
risks.mitigation: Use a separate execution gate with job/resource ceilings and deletion receipts.

risks.failure_mode: Refusals, invalid outputs, or inconclusive answers hide poor recall or poor specificity.
risks.mitigation: Count nondecisions separately and prevent them from contributing to TP or TN counts.
